description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two wide, dark metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood surface is textured with visible grain and has a weathered appearance, featuring several irregular grayish paint marks or scuffs distributed around its body. The top and bottom are flat and made of the same wood, with the top surface showing faint circular patterns and some additional scuffing. The metal bands appear slightly worn, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The overall look is rustic and utilitarian, typical of barrels used for storage or transport in urban or industrial settings. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings other than the closed top.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with dark metal hoops and gray scuff marks, featuring a rustic, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the barrel's wood grain, two dark metal hoops, and prominent gray scuff marks on the left side.
description_view_1: This view highlights more scuff marks on the right and a slightly more pronounced bulge in the barrel's midsection.
description_view_2: The barrel's side is visible with a long horizontal gray mark and less pronounced wood grain detail.
description_view_3: This angle shows a large gray scuff near the top and a vertical mark, with the metal hoops clearly visible.
